laws.title: Public Law 14-91 | Military Code of Military Justice Reform Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is a shell/proposed-UCMJ text; it does not supply the substantive military-code rewrite needed to fix a lawful Code disposition.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only a short historical note; do not infer any UCMJ rewrite.
provisions.evidence: This act may be referred to as "Uniform Code of Military Justice Reform Act of 2023".

provisions.text_summary: Defines the act by reference to a proposed UCMJ text that is not supplied.
provisions.exact_change: No executable Code text can be derived from the source.
provisions.evidence: shall refer to the "Proposed Uniform Code of Military Justice"

provisions.text_summary: Severability and anti-supersession clause.
provisions.exact_change: Retain only as historical savings language.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall be severable

provisions.text_summary: Directs immediate application of the proposed UCMJ to all branches.
provisions.exact_change: Exclude from Code text; it is an uncodified directive to apply an unspecified proposed UCMJ.
provisions.evidence: shall be immediately applied to all branches of the U.S. Armed Forces

provisions.text_summary: Findings on military-justice review and modernization.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as findings/history only.
provisions.evidence: It is found that the Uniform Code of Military Justice is an essential component

provisions.text_summary: Immediate enactment language.
provisions.exact_change: If retained, add only a short enactment/effective-date note.
provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect upon passage

current_implementation.detail: The branch treats the shell as a permanent-note entry instead of flagging that the substantive UCMJ text is absent.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise historical/source-limited note.
Do not invent any proposed UCMJ text or positive-law placement.

laws.title: Public Law 15-92 | Commerce and Labor Innovation Initiative Act
laws.status_basis: PL 31-226 repeals this law in its entirety before codification, so no current Code insertion survives.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective-date language.
provisions.exact_change: No current Code insertion; preserve only history.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Commerce and Labor Innovation Initiative Act".

provisions.text_summary: General media-license suspension and revocation standards.
provisions.exact_change: Superseded before codification; do not insert as current Code text.
provisions.evidence: The general guidelines for suspension or revocation of media licenses shall be the following

provisions.text_summary: Business-license suspension/revocation procedure.
provisions.exact_change: Superseded before codification; keep only a historical record.
provisions.evidence: The procedure for the suspension or revocation of business licenses shall be the following

current_implementation.placement: no matching note found in current branch XML or origin/main
current_implementation.detail: The law was superseded before codification, and no current XML note or amendment survives.

recommended_actions: Do not insert into the Code.
Leave only superseded-history treatment.

laws.title: Public Law 15-93 | Checks and Balances Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The act is an operative repealer; nothing in the supplied materials repeals PL 15-93 itself, so it remains current as repeal-history legislation.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.exact_change: Keep a concise note only.
provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the "Checks and Balances Act of 2023"

provisions.text_summary: Express repeal of H.R. 10 / Public Law 3-15.
provisions.exact_change: Mark PL 3-15 as repealed in the history chain; do not create positive-law text.
provisions.evidence: H.R. 10 - Admission to United Nations Act or known as Public Law 3-15 shall be repealed in its entirety.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate-effect language.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a short effective-date note.
provisions.evidence: This bill will take effect immediately after passage

current_implementation.detail: The current note is parked in the wrong title and still carries a full quoted-content dump; it should be moved to 2 U.S.C. 271 and explicitly record the repeal of PL 3-15.

recommended_actions: Trim the note to a concise historical entry at 2 U.S.C. 271.
Apply the repeal history to PL 3-15 explicitly.

laws.title: Public Law 15-94 | Whistleblower Protections Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The PDF is image-only/scanned and no recoverable enactment text is available, so the operative statutory content cannot be verified.

provisions.text_summary: Source-limited historical note only; no readable enactment text was recovered.
provisions.exact_change: No verified operative language can be stated.
provisions.evidence: valid PDF; no extractable text (possibly scanned)
provisions.notes: No lawful chronology can be fixed from the supplied record.

current_implementation.detail: A default Title 18 anchor was chosen even though the law is source-limited and no readable enactment text was recovered.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise source-limited historical note.
Remove the Trello link and avoid false precision in the codification prose.

laws.title: Public Law 15-95 | An Act to Repeal the White House Protection Act
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable, so the repeal target and scope cannot be authenticated.

provisions.text_summary: Historical note only; no operative positive-law amendment can be verified.
provisions.exact_change: No operative positive-law amendment can be stated.
provisions.evidence: Source deleted or not found
provisions.notes: The repeal chain cannot be reconstructed from the missing text.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note presents a generic repeal-history entry without transparently flagging that the source itself is missing.

recommended_actions: Retain only a source-limited historical note.
Do not invent operative text or a specific repeal chain.

laws.title: Public Law 15-96 | Prisoner Bail Protection Act
laws.status_basis: PL 16-113 expressly repeals PL 15-96 in full, so the law belongs in history only.

provisions.text_summary: Historical note only; the later repealer removes any surviving operative effect.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only a concise historical note citing the later repeal.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 15-96 shall be repealed in its entirety.

current_implementation.detail: The note is directionally correct, but it should explicitly cite PL 16-113 as the repealer and acknowledge that the original source is missing.

recommended_actions: Keep a concise historical note that cites the later repeal by PL 16-113.
Remove boilerplate and Trello noise.

laws.title: Public Law 15-97 | Uniform Legal Practice Authority Act
laws.status_basis: Title I was later repealed in full, while Title II remains operative and PL 15-105 also partially amended Title I before that repeal.

provisions.text_summary: General provisions: short title, ratification/effective date, and severability.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a statutory note and reflect later-law chronology.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Uniform Legal Practice Authority Act of 2023"

provisions.text_summary: Federal Bar Association abolition, apprenticeship/licensing framework, and ULPA establishment.
provisions.exact_change: History only; Title I is later repealed and specific subsections were partially amended before repeal.
provisions.evidence: Title I - LEGAL EXAMINATION AND CERTIFICATION
provisions.notes: Later law PL 25-184 repeals Title I in full.

provisions.text_summary: District-court magistracy, limitations, jurisdiction, chief-judge duties, and barred-individual enforcement.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as a current statutory note; Title II remains operative.
provisions.evidence: TITLE II - UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT

current_implementation.detail: The current note is boilerplate and does not track the later-law amendments and repeals affecting Title I.

recommended_actions: Replace the boilerplate note with one that records the later amendments and the Title I repeal history.
Keep the enactment as a Title 28 statutory note.

laws.title: Public Law 15-98 | Inspector General Act (2023)
laws.status_basis: The enactment is classified as commemorative/non-Code material, so it remains a public law but does not warrant a U.S. Code insertion.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as statutory-note material.
provisions.evidence: Inspector General Protection Act of 2023

provisions.text_summary: Establishment of Inspector General offices and appointment/removal rules.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as a current statutory note; do not convert to positive-law text.
provisions.evidence: There shall be a head of each Office of Inspector General

provisions.text_summary: Whistleblower protection, reporting duties, and special circumstances.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory-note material.
provisions.evidence: required to give a report to Congress twice a month

provisions.text_summary: Administrative access, documents, subpoena and investigative powers, audits, complaints, and committee reports.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a statutory note covering the OIG powers and procedures.
provisions.evidence: The OIG shall have the authority to issue subpoenas

provisions.text_summary: Duplicate enactment clause at the end of the source.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only a short enactment note if needed.
provisions.evidence: Will be declared law once passed

current_implementation.detail: The note is in the right subject area but still carries a full quoted-content dump, a Trello link, and a misnamed heading.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
Keep it as statutory-note material and correct the heading to Inspector General Protection Act of 2023.

laws.title: Public Law 15-99 | Second National Federal Torts Act
laws.status_basis: The act is a general and permanent tort statute, and nothing in the supplied materials repeals it; it belongs in the Title 28 note chain.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, repeal of PL 2-14, severability, and immediate effectiveness.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as Title 28 note material and explicitly record the repeal of PL 2-14.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 2-14, the (first) National Federal Torts Act is null and void.

provisions.text_summary: Civil-suit initiation, federal jurisdiction, and the enumerated personal tort causes of action.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as statutory-note material covering the civil tort framework.
provisions.evidence: Any person who is a Citizen of the United States of America shall have the ability to launch a civil lawsuit

provisions.text_summary: Government-tort rules, defenses, intervention, and individual-capacity liability.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as statutory-note material covering government-tort procedure and defenses.
provisions.evidence: The Attorney General shall defend all cases against the United States.

current_implementation.detail: The Title 28 anchor is sensible, but the branch still needs to repeal-mark PL 2-14 in the prior note chain.

recommended_actions: Record the repeal of PL 2-14 explicitly.
Keep the act as a Title 28 statutory note.

laws.title: Public Law 15-100 | Employee Rights and Limitations Act (leave, employment, termination)
laws.status_basis: The source text is substantive and permanent, and later amendment PL 17-131 confirms that it remains live rather than repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and definitions.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a Title 5 statutory note.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Employment Rights and Limitations Act.

provisions.text_summary: Employment limitations and citizenship-based eligibility rules.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory-note material.
provisions.evidence: Only persons who are Citizens of the United States are permitted to be employed

provisions.text_summary: Position classifications and clarifying provisions.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory-note material.
provisions.evidence: The following positions are considered "primary" positions

provisions.text_summary: Employment rights: leave, holidays, hours, commitments, termination notice, anti-discrimination, and civil action.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory-note material.
provisions.evidence: Every employee of an agency under the provisions of this Act is entitled to leave

provisions.text_summary: Enforcement, penalties, and supervisor misconduct.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory-note material.
provisions.evidence: The Attorney General shall detect and investigate all offenses against this Act

current_implementation.detail: The branch incorrectly labels the act as repealed or superseded even though the source and later amendment PL 17-131 show it is operative.

recommended_actions: Correct the status to current.
Trim the note to a concise Title 5 history entry.
